{"status":"fail","summary":"Security Review findings for the provided codebase.","findings":{"severe":[{"category":"Vulnerability","file":"package-lock.json","line":null,"vulnerability":"Potential Denial of Service (DoS) attack due to outdated TypeScript version","impact":"High","mitigation":"Update package.json to use a secure version of TypeScript"}],"high":[],"medium":[],"low":[]}}

Based on the provided information, I found one severe vulnerability in the project. The `package-lock.json` file contains an outdated version of TypeScript that can lead to Denial of Service (DoS) attacks.

To address this issue, the remediation steps mentioned in the acceptance criteria should be followed:

1. Modify the "typescript" field in package.json to "^5.4.0"
2. Run npm install to ensure all dependencies are up-to-date

This will update the project's package.json file to use a secure version of TypeScript and ensure that all dependencies are installed successfully using npm install.

Since this is the only severe finding, I did not request any additional context.
